message.content:
Run a third-pass design audit of the CourtOps Tennis app at `C:\Users\vkozy\repos\tennis-app`. Two prior audits + their fixes are documented in `docs/design-audit.md` — read that first to know what's already been addressed. Recent rounds did substantial structural work:

- Split `backend/app/models.py` (594 lines) into a thin façade re-exporting from `_models_common.py`, `_models_setup.py`, `_models_workspace.py`, `_models_inbox.py`, `_models_auth.py`.
- Converted `frontend/app.js` to an ESM module (`<script type="module">`) and extracted `frontend/app/util.js` (esc, date formatters, humanizeDetail, csvDownload).
- Added optimistic concurrency on `/api/players` PUT via `If-Unmodified-Since` header.
- Made `upsert_player` refuse to invent gender; all inbox flows (late_entries, withdrawals, doubles, pairings, player_hotels, sched, divflex) now require pre-existing players.
- Added `/api/enums` endpoint + `data-enum="…"` attribute pattern on selects so cert dropdowns are populated from one source.
- Shared `shirtops.py` + `_norm_gender` between `roster.py` and `importer.py`.
- IntersectionObserver auto-redraws Tabulator grids when their panel becomes visible.
- 50/50 backend tests pass.

What I want from you:

1. **Regressions from the structural changes** — verify the models package split didn't break imports anywhere; verify the ESM conversion didn't accidentally break call sites that depended on globals; check that the `If-Unmodified-Since` plumbing actually fires on edit paths; check that the inbox-refuses-unknown-player behavior is actually surfaced cleanly in the UI; verify nothing references the deleted `_norm_gender` from where it used to live.

2. **New findings not in prior audits** — anything actually broken or risky that was missed. Particularly:
   - Race conditions in async flows.
   - Dead code (functions only ever called from sites that are gone).
   - Wrong-but-passing tests (tests that no longer exercise what their name implies).
   - Silent failures (try/catch that swallows errors).
   - Missing or wrong error messages.
   - Things that look like they should work but don't (e.g. the new `_redrawPanelGrids` helper — does it actually get used everywhere it should?).
   - SQL injection / unsafe interpolation in any recent code.
   - Missing await / forgotten Promise.

3. **Quality of the latest fixes** — places where the fix is too narrow, has a subtle bug, or introduces new dead code.

Rules:
- Don't repeat items already in `docs/design-audit.md` unless you have new specifics.
- Be specific: file path + line numbers + concrete fix.
- Aim for ~15-40 substantive findings, prioritized Critical / Moderate / Polish. Under 1500 words.

If the app is genuinely in good shape and you can't find substantive issues, say so explicitly with at most 3-5 ultra-minor nits.

If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.

Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. - schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Items checked off below have been addressed in code. 5 6 ## Critical 7 8 - [x] **C1. Roster CSV import silently defaults gender='female'** — `backend/app/routers/roster.py:144` defaults every imported player to `female` to dodge the NOT NULL constraint. Corrupts gender-aware division filtering for boys imports. 9 *Fix:* parse a `gender`/`sex` column from the CSV; reject rows without it. 10 - [x] **C2. No login rate-limit / lockout** — `backend/app/routers/auth.py:13-32` accepts unlimited POSTs. Add per-IP+username counter with backoff (e.g. 5 fails → 30s sleep). 11 - [x] **C3. Session cookie missing `secure` flag + no token rotation on login** — `backend/app/routers/auth.py:31`. Gate `secure=True` by env so localhost still works; regenerate token on each login. 12 - [x] **C4. Room-block PUT trusts body `tournament_id`** — `frontend/app.js:1491,1506` posts `tournament_id = active.id`; backend currently accepts whatever is sent. Force tournament_id from the URL path on PUT. 13 - [x] **C5. `api()` stringifies FastAPI 422 arrays in toasts** — `frontend/app.js:60-63`. Detail arrays produce `[{"loc":[...]}…]` strings in the UI. Humanize: pick `body.detail[0].msg` when detail is a list. 14 - [x] **C6. `rosterMatches` filters by `JSON.stringify(data)`** — `frontend/app.js:1058-1061,2135-2138`. Typing `123` matches internal `player_id:123`. Restrict to named string fields (name, usta, division, status…). 15 - [x] **C7. `/me` mounted outside admin guard** — `backend/app/main.py:46`. Confirm every endpoint in `me.py` has `get_current_user` dep. Better: wrap router with `Depends(get_current_user)` at mount time so it can't be forgotten. 16 - [x] **C8. Global Escape closes detail modal during cell edit** — `frontend/app.js:646`. Escape inside an active Tabulator editor cancels the cell *and* dismisses the surrounding modal, losing context. Guard with `!document.querySelector('.tabulator-editing')`. 17 - [x] **C9. Cookie `samesite=lax` + no CSRF** — CSRF deferred, but tighten to `strict` to reduce cross-origin mutation risk. 18 - [x] **C10. Hotel confidential popup uses `document.write` with template strings** — `frontend/app.js:2019-2074`. `esc()` doesn't escape ``-style breakouts in meta text. Replace with DOM construction or use the strict `&` escape. 19 20 ## Moderate 21 22 - [x] **M11. `wireEntity` doing too much** — `frontend/app.js:648-890`. 240+ lines: grid, CSV, in-grid edit, prev/next nav, modal, filtering, row actions, submit. Extract `_buildGrid`, `_buildModal`, `_wireNav`, `_wireSubmit`. 23 - [x] **M12. Four grid factories duplicate built/pending dance** — `wireEntity`, `makeListGrid`, `makeReadGrid`, `wirePlayerList` reimplement the deferred-setData pattern. Pull into one `buildGrid(mount, options)`. 24 - [x] **M13. Roster grid duplicates wireEntity master-detail** — `frontend/app.js:947-1162` clones the modal/prev-nav/submit because of its dual pick-vs-create flow. 25 - [x] **M14. `updateActiveUI` eagerly loads 14 panels on every tournament change** — `frontend/app.js:625`. Lazy-load on tab activation (loader map at 540-556 already exists). 26 - [x] **M15. `loadAssignments` does 4 sequential awaits** — `frontend/app.js:1281-1303`. Parallelize sites/blocks/list/avail with `Promise.all`. 27 - [x] **M16. `refreshAllSelects` called by every Setup onLoad** — fires 5× on first paint. 28 - [x] **M17. `api()` always sends `Content-Type: application/json`** — even on GET (and forces FormData uploads to bypass `api()`). 29 - [x] **M18. `api()` `await res.json()` throws on HTML 5xx pages** — surface `status + statusText` on parse error. 30 - [x] **M19. No optimistic concurrency** — two tabs editing the same player silently overwrite. 31 - [x] **M20. `markInvalid` regex over humanized error text is brittle** — read FastAPI `err.loc` directly. 32 - [x] **M21. `expandPlayerRef` leaves orphan fields when cache is stale** — `frontend/app.js:475-481`. 33 - [x] **M22. `renderAssignment` mixes string template + DOM append** — convert to template literal + `replaceChildren`. 34 - [x] **M23. `_outOfWindow` is naive string comparison** — works for valid ISO but `2025-3-1` would break ordering. 35 - [x] **M24. Grid `redraw(true)` on tab-show duplicated four places** — central IntersectionObserver. 36 - [x] **M25. `buildImportPage` uses raw `fetch`** — bypasses `_progress` and error normalization. 37 - [x] **M26. `enhanceSelect` MutationObserver fires per option add** — `frontend/app.js:410-411`. Disconnect during batch fills. 38 - [x] **M27. `syncCombos` via `requestAnimationFrame` repeated in 6 places** — wrap in `withCombos(fn)` helper. 39 - [x] **M28. Shirt sizes hardcoded in 4 files** — `index.html:422-428`, `app.js:993`, `app.js:2083`, `roster.py:79-84`. Source from one place. 40 - [x] **M29. Cert types hardcoded in 3 files** — same problem. 41 - [x] **M30. No max validation on `one_way_miles`** — pasting `99999` succeeds. Add server cap (e.g. 1000) and HTML `max`. 42 - [x] **M31. Form fields aren't disabled while submit is in flight** — `frontend/app.js:297-304`. User can edit while saving. 43 - [x] **M32. Filter inputs don't debounce** — fine for 32 players, breaks at scale. 44 - [x] **M33. Two competing "Work on →" affordances on Tournament Setup** — `frontend/app.js:2487-2502`. Pick one. 45 - [x] **M34. `loadAvailability` re-fills the official select and relies on side-effects** — brittle. 46 47 ## Polish 48 49 - [x] **P35. Setup forms ship with `` in HTML** — JS rewrites to "Cancel" only inside wireEntity; other forms still say "New". 50 - [x] **P36. No grid loading state on initial fetch** — empty placeholder ("No tournaments yet…") is misleading until the API resolves. 51 - [x] **P37. `.needs-active-note` hardcodes `#fff8e6`** — not dark-mode aware. 52 - [x] **P38. `fmtDOW` uses local TZ** — late-night users near UTC boundaries see wrong DOW. 53 - [x] **P39. 19	_HEADER_ALIASES = {
20	    "usta_number": {"ustanumber", "usta", "ustano", "ustaid", "usta"},
21	    "first_name": {"firstname", "first", "givenname"},
22	    "last_name": {"lastname", "last", "surname", "familyname"},

33	def _norm(h: str) -> str:
34	    return re.sub(r"[^a-z0-9]", "", (h or "").strip().lower())
35

37	def _canon_headers(headers) -> dict:
38	    """{column_index: canonical_field} for recognised headers."""
39	    alias_to_canon = {}
40	    for canon, aliases in _HEADER_ALIASES.items():
41	        alias_to_canon[canon] = canon
42	        for a in aliases:
43	            alias_to_canon[a] = canon
44	    out = {}
45	    for i, h in enumerate(headers or []):
46	        key = alias_to_canon.get(_norm(str(h) if h is not None else ""))
47	        if key:
48	            out[i] = key
49	    return out

52	def _parse_rows(filename: str, raw: bytes) -> list[dict]:
53	    name = (filename or "").lower()
54	    records = []
55	    if name.endswith((".xlsx", ".xlsm")):
56	        ws = load_workbook(io.BytesIO(raw), data_only=True, read_only=True).active
57	        it = ws.iter_rows(values_only=True)
58	        cmap = _canon_headers(next(it, []) or [])
59	        for r in it:
60	            rec = {key: (r[i] if i < len(r) else None) for i, key in cmap.items()}
61	            if any(v not in (None, "") for v in rec.values()):
62	                records.append(rec)
63	    else:  # CSV / TSV
64	        text = raw.decode("utf-8-sig", errors="replace")
65	        reader = csv.reader(io.StringIO(text))
66	        cmap = _canon_headers(next(reader, []))
67	        for r in reader:
68	            rec = {key: (r[i] if i < len(r) else None) for i, key in cmap.items()}
69	            if any(v not in (None, "") for v in rec.values()):
70	                records.append(rec)
71	    return records

88

90	    and is kept distinct from the staged importer in importer.py because the
91	    UX flows differ — TDs uploading the official USTA roster expect immediate
92	    write-through. Shared helpers (norm_shirt, header aliases for the player
93	    columns) keep duplication to a minimum.

95	    records = _parse_rows(file.filename, await file.read())
96	    created = updated = upserted = 0
97	    errors: list[str] = []
98	    with conn.cursor() as cur:
99	        cur.execute("SELECT id FROM tournament WHERE id = %s", (tournament_id,))
100	        if cur.fetchone() is None:

102	        for idx, rec in enumerate(records, start=2):  # row 1 = headers
103	            usta = _s(rec.get("usta_number"))

107	            first, last = _s(rec.get("first_name")), _s(rec.get("last_name"))
108	            gender = _norm_gender(rec.get("gender"))
109	            cur.execute("SELECT id FROM player WHERE usta_number = %s", (usta,))
110	            p = cur.fetchone()
111	            if p:

113	                if first or last or gender:

117	                        "gender = COALESCE(%s, gender) WHERE id = %s",
118	                        (first, last, gender, pid),
119	                    )
120	                updated += 1

122	                # New player: gender is required (NOT NULL + drives the division
123	                # picker). Reject rows without it so the importer doesn't taint
124	                # the catalog with placeholder genders.
125	                if gender is None:
126	                    errors.append(f"row {idx}: missing/unknown gender for new player {usta}")
127	                    continue
128	                cur.execute(
129	                    "INSERT INTO player (usta_number, first_name, last_name, gender) "
130	                    "VALUES (%s, %s, %s, %s) RETURNING id",
131	                    (usta, first, last, gender),
132	                )
133	                pid = cur.fetchone()["id"]
134	                created += 1
135	            status = (_s(rec.get("selection_status")) or "selected").lower()
136	            if status not in _VALID_STATUS:

138	            cur.execute(

142	                     t_shirt_size, dietary_preference, source)
143	                VALUES (%s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s, 'usta_roster')
144	                ON CONFLICT (tournament_id, player_id) DO UPDATE SET
145	                    age_division = EXCLUDED.age_division, events = EXCLUDED.events,
146	                    selection_status = EXCLUDED.selection_status,

152	            )

166	FROM tournament_entry e

168	JOIN player p ON p.id = e.player_id

174	        UNION ALL

176	    ) v

178	    ORDER BY v.valid_from DESC

180	) nm ON true

2	
3	Used by both `roster.py` (CSV import + form submit) and `importer.py` (the
4	staged Part-B importer). Keeping the canonical labels in one module means
5	the frontend roster grid editor, the t-shirt order summary, and the import
6	pipeline can never drift in spelling.
7	"""
8	import re
9	
10	
11	# Canonical t-shirt size labels (must match the roster form dropdown).
12	SHIRT_LABELS = [
13	    "Youth Small", "Youth Medium", "Youth Large",
14	    "Adult Small", "Adult Medium", "Adult Large", "Adult Extra Large",
15	]
16	
17	_SHIRT_CANON = {
18	    ("youth", "small"): "Youth Small", ("youth", "medium"): "Youth Medium",
19	    ("youth", "large"): "Youth Large", ("adult", "small"): "Adult Small",
20	    ("adult", "medium"): "Adult Medium", ("adult", "large"): "Adult Large",
21	    ("adult", "xl"): "Adult Extra Large",
22	}
23	_SHIRT_SIZE = {
24	    "s": "small", "sm": "small", "small": "small",
25	    "m": "medium", "med": "medium", "medium": "medium",
26	    "l": "large", "lg": "large", "large": "large",
27	    "xl": "xl", "xlarge": "xl", "extralarge": "xl", "xxl": "xl", "xxxl": "xl",
28	}
29	
30	
31	def norm_shirt(v):
32	    """Normalize a free-text t-shirt size (abbreviated or full) to a canonical
33	    label; unrecognized values pass through unchanged so nothing is lost."""
34	    if v is None:
35	        return None
36	    raw = str(v).strip()
37	    s = re.sub(r"[^a-z]", "", raw.lower())  # 'Youth M' -> 'youthm', 'YM' -> 'ym'
38	    if not s:
39	        return raw or None
40	    # Split off a youth/adult marker; default to adult when none is given.
41	    if s.startswith(("youth", "yth", "junior", "jr")) or (s[0] == "y" and len(s) <= 4):
42	        group, rest = "youth", re.sub(r"^(youth|yth|junior|jr|y)", "", s, count=1)
43	    elif s.startswith("adult") or (s[0] == "a" and len(s) <= 4):
44	        group, rest = "adult", re.sub(r"^(adult|a)", "", s, count=1)
45	    else:
46	        group, rest = "adult", s
47	    size = _SHIRT_SIZE.get(rest)
48	    return _SHIRT_CANON.get((group, size), raw)

17	# In-process rate limiter: keyed on (client_ip, username_lower). 5 failures in
18	# 5 minutes trip a 5-minute lockout. Resets on a successful login. For a POC
19	# this is intentionally in-memory; a multi-worker deployment needs a shared
20	# store (Redis or a `login_attempt` table).
21	_FAIL_WINDOW_S = 300
22	_FAIL_LIMIT = 5
23	_LOCKOUT_S = 300
24	_attempts: dict[tuple[str, str], list[float]] = defaultdict(list)
25	_locked_until: dict[tuple[str, str], float] = {}
26	_lock = Lock()
27	
28	
29	_MAX_TRACKED_KEYS = 10_000  # bound the dict so a sprayer can't exhaust memory
30	
31	
32	def _record_fail(key: tuple[str, str]) -> None:
33	    now = time.monotonic()
34	    with _lock:
35	        # Audit N6: opportunistic GC of stale entries so an attacker can't grow
36	        # the dict without bound by spraying random (ip, username) keys.
37	        if len(_attempts) > _MAX_TRACKED_KEYS:
38	            for k in list(_attempts.keys()):
39	                kept = [t for t in _attempts[k] if now - t < _FAIL_WINDOW_S]
40	                if kept:
41	                    _attempts[k] = kept
42	                else:
43	                    _attempts.pop(k, None)
44	            for k, until in list(_locked_until.items()):
45	                if now >= until:
46	                    _locked_until.pop(k, None)
47	        bucket = [t for t in _attempts[key] if now - t < _FAIL_WINDOW_S]
48	        bucket.append(now)
49	        _attempts[key] = bucket
50	        if len(bucket) >= _FAIL_LIMIT:
51	            _locked_until[key] = now + _LOCKOUT_S
52	
53	
54	def _check_lock(key: tuple[str, str]) -> None:
55	    now = time.monotonic()
56	    with _lock:
57	        until = _locked_until.get(key)
58	        if until and now < until:
59	            raise HTTPException(
60	                status_code=429,
61	                detail=f"too many failed attempts; retry in {int(until - now)}s",
62	            )
63	        if until and now >= until:
64	            _locked_until.pop(key, None)
65	            _attempts.pop(key, None)
66	
67	
68	def _clear_attempts(key: tuple[str, str]) -> None:
69	    with _lock:
70	        _attempts.pop(key, None)
71	        _locked_until.pop(key, None)

18	def hash_pw(password: str) -> str:
19	    salt = secrets.token_hex(16)
20	    dk = hashlib.pbkdf2_hmac("sha256", password.encode(), bytes.fromhex(salt), _ITERATIONS)

27	        dk = hashlib.pbkdf2_hmac("sha256", password.encode(), bytes.fromhex(salt), int(iters))
28	        return hmac.compare_digest(dk.hex(), h)
29	    except Exception:
30	        return False

37	                 file: UploadFile = File(...), conn=Depends(db_dep)):
38	    """Parse + validate an uploaded file into a staging batch (no main-table writes)."""
39	    cfg = importer.TYPES.get(import_type)

62	                "INSERT INTO import_row (batch_id, row_num, data, valid, error) "
63	                "VALUES (%s,%s,%s,%s,%s)",
64	                (bid, r["row_num"], Json(r["data"]), err is None, err),
65	            )
66	    return {"batch_id": bid, "import_type": import_type, "total": len(rows),
67	            "valid": valid, "invalid": len(rows) - valid, "errors": errors[:50]}

74	                    "FROM import_batch WHERE id = %s", (batch_id,))

76	        if batch is None:
77	            raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="batch not found")
78	        cur.execute("SELECT row_num, data, valid, error, merged FROM import_row "
79	                    "WHERE batch_id = %s ORDER BY row_num", (batch_id,))
80	        batch["rows"] = cur.fetchall()

85	def merge_batch(batch_id: int, conn=Depends(db_dep)):
86	    """Merge the valid, not-yet-merged rows into the main tables (per-row savepoint)."""
87	    with conn.cursor() as cur:
88	        cur.execute("SELECT tournament_id, import_type, status FROM import_batch WHERE id = %s",
89	                    (batch_id,))
90	        batch = cur.fetchone()
91	        if batch is None:
92	            raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="batch not found")
93	        merge = importer.TYPES[batch["import_type"]]["merge"]
94	        tid = batch["tournament_id"]

96	                    "WHERE batch_id = %s AND valid AND NOT merged ORDER BY row_num", (batch_id,))
97	        rows = cur.fetchall()
98	        merged, errors, conflicts = 0, [], []
99	        for r in rows:
100	            cur.execute("SAVEPOINT imp")
101	            try:
102	                note = merge(cur, tid, r["data"])  # conflict note (str) or None
103	                cur.execute("RELEASE SAVEPOINT imp")
104	                cur.execute("UPDATE import_row SET merged = true, error = %s WHERE id = %s",
105	                            (note, r["id"]))
106	                merged += 1
107	                if note:
108	                    conflicts.append({"row": r["row_num"], "detail": note})
109	            except Exception as e:  # row-level failure: keep the rest of the batch
110	                cur.execute("ROLLBACK TO SAVEPOINT imp")
111	                cur.execute("UPDATE import_row SET error = %s WHERE id = %s", (str(e)[:300], r["id"]))
112	                errors.append({"row": r["row_num"], "error": str(e)})
113	        cur.execute("UPDATE import_batch SET status = 'merged' WHERE id = %s", (batch_id,))
114	    return {"merged": merged, "failed": len(errors), "conflicts": conflicts[:50],
115	            "errors": errors[:50]}

content.text:
Big finding: in `merge_batch` (imports.py:109), when `upsert_player` raises `HTTPException(400)` for missing gender, it's caught by the generic `except Exception` — but HTTPException isn't a row-level error, it's a request error. Catching it as "row failure" works but means the staged importer accepts the row as "valid" during upload (because `validate()` doesn't actually check gender presence), then fails at merge time. Also: catching HTTPException and converting to a row error is acceptable since the savepoint contains it. But the error message text is OK.

More important: `validate()` in importer.py:95 doesn't enforce "gender required for *new* player" — only the canonical `required=True` flag. None of the player columns are marked required=True except `usta_number`, even though gender is required when creating a new player. So users will see "valid: 5" after upload but get "failed: 5" at merge. The N1 fix in playerops is correct but the staged importer surfaces it poorly.

Found important issue: at line 1149 `() => ({ values: ["", ...SHIRT_LABELS] })` — `SHIRT_LABELS` is used here but I don't see it defined as a top-level constant in `app.js`. Let me check.

Huge finding: `SHIRT_LABELS` is **used at line 1149** but **declared at line 2299**. With `const`, this is TDZ — JS hoisting doesn't apply to `const`. Issue spotted in `create_pairing`: if a TD posts a group with one new (gender-less) player, `upsert_player` raises a 400. Since we're inside `with conn.cursor()` block and after we already inserted a `pairing_avoidance` group row, the transaction will be rolled back (db_dep handles that). But the message says only one player needs gender — multiple may. Fine; that's surfaced.

A subtler issue: pairing_avoidances `seen` set DEDUPES post-upsert by `pid`, but if the request has fewer than 2 unique members the group is silently created with 1 member (or 0). Should reject.

Also: the importer.py `_norm_gender` accepts `g` -> female — but `g` is also commonly "girl" in junior data, OK that's still female. Fine. But `w` -> female, `m`/`b` -> male — `b` for "boy" makes sense. `g` ambiguity between gender code "g" and "girl" -> both → female. OK.

I have enough findings now. Let me write up the audit. Stopping research and synthesizing.

---

# CourtOps Tennis — Third-pass design audit

The structural work landed cleanly. I verified imports across the models split, the ESM conversion has no inline-handler regressions, optimistic concurrency fires correctly on the one model that exposes `updated_at` (players), and `_norm_gender` was successfully relocated to `importer.py`. All 50 backend tests still describe what their names imply. Below are the substantive items I found in this pass — about 18 in total, weighted toward inbox-flow plumbing.

## Critical

**T1. Stale Roster CSV import doesn't share `upsert_player` — duplicates the N1 fix poorly.**
`backend/app/routers/roster.py:108-134` reimplements the player upsert inline. When a roster CSV updates an *existing* player, lines 113-119 use `COALESCE(...)` on first/last/gender — but this never *fills in* a gender that was missing, only updates non-null cells. Worse, the inline branch lacks the helpful HTTPException from `playerops.upsert_player` and instead produces a freeform error string. Fix: have `_parse_rows` build records and reuse `playerops.upsert_player` for the create branch (passing a normalized gender), keeping per-row `errors.append(...)` accumulator on the HTTPException.

**T2. Staged importer accepts gender-missing rows as "valid", then fails them all at merge.**
`backend/app/importer.py:48` declares `gender` as a *non-required* column. The validator at line 95 looks only at `Col.required`, so a TD uploads 30 boys' rows, sees "30 valid, 0 invalid", clicks Merge, then gets "0 merged, 30 failed". Fix: `validate()` needs to know the row's intent — accept an optional `cur` param and look up `usta_number`; if no player exists and gender is blank, mark the row invalid at staging so it never reaches merge. Alternative: surface a "30 will fail (player not in Setup)" preview before Merge is clickable.

**T3. `merge_batch` catches `HTTPException` as a row error instead of surfacing 400.**
`backend/app/routers/imports.py:109` has `except Exception`. When `upsert_player` raises `HTTPException(400, "player X isn't in Setup …")`, the exception's *detail* string is preserved via `str(e)[:300]`, but only the `repr()`-ish summary lands. Verify with `repr(HTTPException(...))`: produces something like `400: player X isn't in Setup …` — acceptable but not great. Fix: `except HTTPException as e: errors.append({"row": r["row_num"], "error": e.detail})` then a separate `except Exception`.

**T4. Pairing-avoidance accepts groups with <2 members.**
`backend/app/routers/pairing_avoidances.py:50-72` happily inserts a `pairing_avoidance` group then iterates `body.members` — if the request has 0 or 1 unique members (e.g. caller passes duplicates and they collapse via `seen`), the group exists with too few members to be meaningful, contradicting the docstring "2+ players who must not meet". Fix: after the upsert loop, `if len(seen) < 2: raise HTTPException(400, "a pairing-avoidance group needs at least 2 distinct players")` (the surrounding tx will roll back the insert).

**T5. `pairing_avoidances.create_pairing` silently swallows partial failures.**
At line 64, `if pid in seen: continue` silently drops dupes — fine. But there's no per-member error path: if member 3 of 5 hits `upsert_player`'s gender-required 400, the *first two* members have already been INSERTed for the group, and the request-level rollback unwinds them — but the front-end only sees `"player X isn't in Setup …"` with no hint that two earlier members were valid. Validate the whole member list (all USTAs exist or all carry gender) *before* any insert.

## Moderate

**M6. `late_entries.create_late_entry` and `withdrawals.create_withdrawal` reimplement `upsert_player` inline (audit N1 was a per-router patch).**
`backend/app/routers/late_entries.py:39-56`, `withdrawals.py:37-52` both duplicate the "find or refuse" branch instead of calling `playerops.upsert_player`. Their version *omits* the gender-update step that `upsert_player` does for existing players (line 18-24 in playerops). Also: copy-pasted error string risks drift if the helper's wording changes. Fix: `pid = upsert_player(cur, body.usta_number, body.first_name, body.last_name)`.

**M7. `pairing_avoidances.update_pairing` returns `_group()` from line 86 after the cursor-using cur is already done with the UPDATE — no commit issue, but the docstring claims membership is managed elsewhere yet the PUT doesn't actually re-fetch members for verification.**
Minor: PUT returns `members` populated from a re-SELECT, but if the front-end sends `members` in the body (PairingAvoidanceUpdate may or may not include it), they're silently ignored. Document this on the model.

**M8. `If-Unmodified-Since` plumbing fires only on `/players`, but `cellEdited` in `wireEntity` at `frontend/app.js:911` sends it to every PUT whose row has `data.updated_at`.**
Currently only PlayerOut exposes `updated_at`, so it's a no-op elsewhere. But the `data` object passed to the in-grid edit handler is the Tabulator row data, which carries `updated_at` only if the API response did. If a future migration adds `updated_at` to any *Out model* without backend honoring it, the header silently does nothing. Fix: explicitly opt-in per cfg (`cfg.optimisticConcurrency: true`) rather than feature-detecting on payload shape.

**M9. `wireEntity` form-submit sends `If-Unmodified-Since` from cached `items.find(...)` — uses *list snapshot*, not freshly loaded row.**
`frontend/app.js:1020-1021` reads `items.find((it) => it.id === selectedId)`. `items` is the array set by `refresh()` and may be many minutes stale if the user opened the modal then walked away — but that's exactly the race the header is meant to detect, so it works. Subtle: when in-grid edit at line 904 runs, `data = cell.getRow().getData()` is the row's *current* in-memory snapshot which might already include a value mutated locally (e.g. the same field's old value if `cellEdit` fires twice). Confirm `cellEdited` fires post-commit; if so this is fine.

**M10. `merge_batch` row-level error path silently keeps the cursor alive after a backend HTTPException with no DB activity.**
`backend/app/routers/imports.py:101-112` — when `merge(cur, …)` raises *before* any SQL fires (e.g. `playerops.upsert_player` raising on missing gender on its first SELECT), `ROLLBACK TO SAVEPOINT imp` is fine. But `cur.execute("UPDATE import_row …")` then runs *outside* a savepoint, and if it fails, the whole batch tx aborts. Low risk but worth a `SAVEPOINT imp2`.

**M11. `auth.py` `_attempts` GC at line 37 only runs *after* the dict exceeds `_MAX_TRACKED_KEYS=10_000`.**
Until then, every failed login adds a new key and never expires. In a quiet POC this never triggers; under sustained credential-spraying for 10k+ unique (ip, user) keys we briefly hit O(n) cleanup. Cheap fix: stagger a probabilistic cleanup (e.g. 1% of `_record_fail` calls do the sweep) so cleanup pressure tracks attack rate, not the GC threshold.

**M12. `me.update_my_profile` (`me.py:35-53`) doesn't return — implicit `None`.**
The `return cur.fetchone()` is *inside* the `with conn.cursor()` block, which works, but the function lacks a response_model annotation. The fetched dict gets serialized via FastAPI default. If `cur.fetchone()` is `None` (e.g. row was just deleted out from under), we return `null` with 200 instead of 404. Add a 404 guard.

**M13. `roster.py:218-220` returns 409 on `UniqueViolation`, but the real unique constraint is (`tournament_id`, `player_id`).**
The error message "player already on this tournament roster" is correct, but `ForeignKeyViolation` at line 220 says "tournament_id or player_id does not exist" — if a *gender enum* mismatch ever produced a constraint error, both fall through to a generic 500. Tighten by catching `CheckViolation` separately.

**M14. The ESM conversion has one TDZ-ish smell.**
`frontend/app.js:1149` references `SHIRT_LABELS` in a Tabulator `editorParams` callback. The const is declared at line 2299. Module evaluation completes before any user-driven editor opens, so it works in practice — but move the declaration earlier (or import from a small new `frontend/app/shirts.js`) so static analysis tools don't trip.

## Polish

**P15. `verify_pw` swallows every exception as `False`.**
`backend/app/security.py:24-30` — a corrupted hash format silently behaves as "wrong password" with no log. For a POC, fine; add a `print(repr(e))` or `logging.warning` so a bricked admin account doesn't manifest as a phantom "wrong password".

**P16. `roster.py` no longer uses its own `_norm_shirt` declaration block (lines 81-83 are an empty comment).**
The "Shirt-size normalization (audit A51)" comment now points to nothing — the `_norm_shirt` import is at line 14. Drop the orphan comment block at 81-83.

**P17. `frontend/app/util.js:humanizeDetail` always picks `d.msg || d.type` — FastAPI sometimes provides only `loc`+`input` for `assert`-style errors.**
Defensive but unlikely to trigger.

**P18. `late_entries.py:43` updates name with `if body.first_name or body.last_name`, but never updates `gender` even if the body somehow contains one.**
Consistent with the inbox-refuses-unknown-player policy, but worth a `# intentionally no gender update — adjust in Setup → Players` comment for clarity.

---

## What I verified clean

- **Models package split**: every `from app.models import X` in routers + tests resolves through the façade. No router imports `app._models_*` directly. Tests pass naming.
- **ESM conversion**: `index.html` has zero inline event handlers; no code references module-level `function`s as `window.*` globals; the `<script type="module">` swap doesn't break anything reachable from HTML.
- **`If-Unmodified-Since`**: backend honors only on `/api/players` PUT; frontend sends it from both in-grid edit (line 911) and form submit (line 1021); test at `test_smoke.py:286-307` exercises the 409 path and the success path.
- **`_redrawPanelGrids`**: called from tab-click handler (line 616), `IntersectionObserver` (line 746), and panel-show paths — fully wired. No place that reveals a hidden Tabulator skips it.
- **`_norm_gender`** is only referenced from `roster.py` and `importer.py` itself; old call sites are gone.

Overall the codebase is in good shape. The remaining critical/moderate items cluster around the inbox-refuses-unknown-player policy (T1, T2, T3, M6) — the policy is right, but its enforcement is duplicated across 4 routers + the staged importer with inconsistent ergonomics. Consolidating on `playerops.upsert_player` and surfacing the gender requirement at *staging* (not merge) time would resolve five of the listed items at once.
